qa_pairs.question: What happens to files when they are archived if they originate from a Lustre filesystem, commonly found on compute clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: When files from a Lustre filesystem (typically found in `/home`, `/project`, or `/scratch` on general-purpose compute clusters) are archived, some extended attributes are saved automatically.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user view the extended attributes associated with files within a `dar` archive that were originally from a Lustre filesystem?
qa_pairs.answer: To see which extended attributes are assigned to each archived file, a user can use the `-alist-ea` flag with the `dar -l` command, for example: `dar -l all -alist-ea`.
